howItWorks.intro: Propane contains 91,502 BTU per gallon. By dividing the total BTUs available in your tank by your appliance's BTU consumption rate, you get the total runtime in hours.
rules: Runtime (hours) = (Tank gallons × Fill % × 91,502) / BTU per hour
A standard 20 lb grill tank holds 4.7 gallons of propane
Large tanks are typically filled to 80% capacity for safety (thermal expansion)
howItWorks.outro: Actual runtime varies with temperature (propane is less efficient in cold weather), altitude, and appliance efficiency. These calculations assume sea level and moderate temperatures.

faq.question: How many BTU are in a gallon of propane?
faq.answer: One gallon of propane contains 91,502 BTU. One pound of propane contains approximately 21,548 BTU. A standard 20 lb tank holds about 4.7 gallons, giving it about 430,000 BTU total.

faq.question: How long does a 20 lb propane tank last on a grill?
faq.answer: At medium heat (about 30,000 BTU), a full 20 lb tank lasts about 14 hours. At full blast (60,000 BTU), it lasts about 7 hours. Actual time depends on how many burners you use and your heat setting.

faq.question: Why are tanks only filled to 80%?
faq.answer: Propane expands as temperature rises. The 20% headspace allows for thermal expansion without creating dangerous pressure. This 80% rule is a safety standard enforced by law.

faq.question: Does cold weather affect propane runtime?
faq.answer: Yes. In cold weather, propane pressure drops and the tank may not deliver fuel as efficiently. Below 0°F, a nearly empty tank may not produce enough pressure to run appliances. Runtime itself isn't reduced, but delivery can be affected.

faq.question: How do I know my appliance's BTU rating?
faq.answer: Check the appliance's data plate (usually on the back or bottom), the owner's manual, or the manufacturer's website. BTU ratings are listed as BTU/hr and represent maximum input.

sections.title: What Is the Propane Tank Runtime Calculator?
sections.body: The Propane Tank Runtime Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your tank size, custom tank, fill level, you get instant results including runtime, usable propane, total btu available. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting runtime right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your tank size and need to find the right runtime. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Tank Size
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your tank size — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: custom tank, fill level, appliance btu rating, custom btu/hour. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your runtime, usable propane, total btu available. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your tank size before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both runtime and usable propane — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your custom btu/hour, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
